raw_findings.content: from transformers import Trainer, TrainingArguments, AutoModelForCausalLM

provider_profile.description: Claude model family API. Used for text generation, analysis, code, and agentic tasks.
provider_profile.enterprise_note: Enterprise plan with DPA, SSO, admin controls, no training on data.
provider_profile.free_tier_risk: Personal claude.ai usage may contribute data to training. API usage does not by default.
provider_profile.name: anthropic
provider_profile.training_policy: API: data not used for training by default. Free tier (claude.ai): data may be used. Enterprise: contractual guarantees.

recommendations: Move API keys to environment variables or a secret manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault).
Rotate the exposed key(s) immediately — they may already be compromised if the repository has been shared or is public.
Add API key patterns to your .gitignore and pre-commit hooks.
Verify GDPR compliance: ensure a DPA is signed with the provider and data residency is configured for EU region if required.
Consider enterprise tier: Enterprise plan with DPA, SSO, admin controls, no training on data.
Confirm which tier/plan is being used. Switch to a paid API or enterprise plan with contractual opt-out from data training.

risk_reasons.detail: Found 1 API key directly in code (sample_py.py). Anyone with repository access can extract and misuse these keys. Keys should be stored in environment variables or a secret manager.
risk_reasons.title: Hardcoded API key in source code

risk_reasons.detail: Anthropic (Claude) processes data in: US. If your organization is subject to GDPR or data residency requirements, verify that a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place and data residency is configured appropriately.
risk_reasons.title: Data may leave the EU

risk_reasons.detail: Anthropic (Claude) training policy: API: data not used for training by default. Free tier (claude.ai): data may be used. Enterprise: contractual guarantees. Verify which tier/plan is being used and whether data opt-out is configured.
risk_reasons.title: Data may be used for model training

risk_reasons.detail: This integration sends data to Anthropic (Claude) (Anthropic, PBC) for processing. Data is transmitted outside your infrastructure. Vendor: Anthropic, PBC.
risk_reasons.title: Data sent to external AI API

insights.summary: You are Fleurdin, a helpful florist assistant specialized in essential oils and aromatherapy. API endpoints: chat(). Reads from: database, database (MESSAGES). Outputs to: external API ().

provider_profile.description: OpenAI models (GPT-4, GPT-4o, embeddings) hosted in Azure tenants with private networking, separate DPA and regional deployment control.
provider_profile.enterprise_note: Private endpoints, VNet integration, Customer-Managed Keys, Azure AD auth, content filtering configurable.
provider_profile.free_tier_risk: No free tier. Access requires an Azure subscription and Microsoft application approval.
provider_profile.name: azure_openai
provider_profile.training_policy: Data is NOT sent to OpenAI and is NOT used to train models. Stays within the customer's Azure tenant.

recommendations: Review what data is being sent to the API. Ensure no PII, confidential, or regulated data is transmitted without appropriate controls.

risk_reasons.detail: This integration sends data to Azure OpenAI (Microsoft Azure) for processing. Data is transmitted outside your infrastructure. Vendor: Microsoft Azure.
risk_reasons.title: Data sent to external AI API

risk_reasons.detail: This asset appears to fine-tuning a model. Training pipelines typically consume real production data (logs, customer messages, documents), so they carry higher privacy, IP-leakage and compute-cost risk than plain inference. Verify data provenance, access controls on the training set, and that resulting model weights are stored in a governed artifact repository.
risk_reasons.title: Fine-tuning pipeline ingests training data

risk_reasons.detail: Model Context Protocol server(s) give an LLM application privileged access to local tools, filesystems or remote APIs. Each configured server is a potential data-exfiltration vector and should be audited individually. Configured servers: filesystem, github.
risk_reasons.title: MCP servers expose tool access to the model

risk_reasons.detail: Docker/Compose files declare an AI runtime or vector store: Ollama runtime, Qdrant vector DB, vLLM inference server. Self-hosting keeps data on-prem but requires you to patch the runtime, monitor resource use, and secure the management endpoints.
risk_reasons.title: Self-hosted AI runtime detected in container manifest

risk_reasons.detail: Found 1 model weight file(s) (.gguf/.safetensors/.onnx/…) tracked in source control. Large binaries bloat the repo and are often better served via a model registry or Git LFS; verify licensing of the underlying model.
risk_reasons.title: Local model weights committed to repository

insights.solution_name: An internal support assistant for the FleurCorp helpdesk
insights.summary: Role: "an internal support assistant for the FleurCorp helpdesk". Key functions: answer. AI calls: client.chat.completions.create.
